2799.name: Submerged Passage
2799.description: Small, phosphorescent fish dart about, giving off an unerring glow that serves well enough to allow you to find your way about. The passage continues to the south, or you may ascend the stairs and return to the banks of the river.

2800.name: Submerged Passage
2800.description: Deep inside the cavern, you cannot help but marvel at the magic that lets you remain alive in the depths. To the west, the pull of the current seems amazingly strong, and you must grab hold of an outcropping of rock to keep from being swept away.

npcs.name: Liquid ooze
npcs.description: Overcome by curiosity, you poke your finger at its soft body and stare in fascination as it emerges from the other side...

2801.name: Skeletal Stomping Grounds
2801.description: Thousands of bone fragments litter the floor here, crunching and grinding beneath your booted feet as you gingerly step over the precarious ground. A second cavern branches off to the south, or you may leave the bone fields by heading west.

npcs.name: Decaying skeleton
npcs.description: Bits of bone crack and break off as the skeleton pulls itself from the cavern floor.  It pauses to replace an arm lost in the process, and then

npcs.name: Hulking skeleton
npcs.description: Splintered legs of gleaming white bone lurch unsteadily along the rough cavern floor, and palsied hands emerge from a shabby robe to wave about in a nefarious chant as the creature advances towards you.

npcs.name: Ragged skeleton
npcs.description: A grinning human skull set atop a pile of ensorcelled bones fixes you with a malevolent stare and begins a slow, inexorable advance.

2802.name: Skeletal Stomping Grounds
2802.description: Like the adjoining cavern, the floor here has a liberal helping of bones strewn about, and you can't help but wonder where in the world all these came from. As if in answer to your question, a skeletal hand pushes its way up from the floor. Dark, twisting roots emerge from cracked stone, pulsing with unsettling energy.

npcs.name: Decaying skeleton
npcs.description: Bits of bone crack and break off as the skeleton pulls itself from the cavern floor.  It pauses to replace an arm lost in the process, and then

2803.name: Before the Cavern
2803.description: The silence in this small passage is deafening. You glance about, and a feeling of dread creeps up your spine as you notice the absolute barrenness of the rock here. Not even a bit of algae can be seen clinging tenaciously to the walls. You may either return to the passage to the east, or brave the cavern to the south.

2804.name: Behemoths Cavern
2804.description: Some massive beast has made a home for itself here, far beneath the river. You marvel at the sheer force it must have taken to thrust the solid rock walls outward and hollow out such an immense cavern. Far back at the end of the room, the tail of an enormous worm can be seen dwindling far off into the shadows.

npcs.name: Behemoth
npcs.description: from the thing's head and blinks slowly in recognition.  With terrible slowness, the great beast shoulders aside the rock wall and stomps

2805.name: Within the Rock
2805.description: Wealth beyond measure lays scattered at your feet and in piles in the corners. Greedily, you start forward to take a gleaming sword of solid gold, wrought with fine silver, but find it frozen beneath a hard outer covering. Casting frantically about the room, you search for anything not covered by the secretions of the behemoth, long since hardened as firm as any rock. Screaming in rage, you beat your fists against a glittering mound of gold and howl in impotent fury at your inability to break the covering.

2818.name: The Downward Spiral
2818.description: Trudging wearily down the stairs, you suddenly notice that the stairs leading down, like those on the way up, hug the wall very close. On a tower of this size, that must leave a lot of empty space between the two flights. . .

2796.name: River Beneath the House of Hashak
2796.description: A dark river rushes past, flowing westward. For a moment you are lost in thought, wondering as to how this river was created, when an eye on a long, thin stalk emerges from the water and brings your attention back to reality.

npcs.name: River fish
npcs.description: Small and very dark, it blends perfectly with the murky waters.  The river fish turns to regard you with a lazy eye, and then does its best to

2797.name: On the River
2797.description: You paddle wearily along the dark, interminable river for what seems like hours. Your breathing becomes ragged and irregular, aching muscles cry out in protest at every stroke, and you want nothing more than to simply give in to the river and let it take you where it may.

e.to_room_name: River Beneath the House of Hashak

npcs.name: Zedar the bottom-dweller
npcs.description: beast.  Zedar rises slowly above the berm, fixes you with an evil stare,

2798.name: Subterranean Stair
2798.description: Gasping for breath, you crawl on hands and knees onto the bank and heave yourself into a small alcove scarely visible behind a clump of dense brush. Nearby, a small stairway leads down to a passage long since flooded when the river swelled over the bank and plunged down into the cavern. You have lost 1040 experience.

2816.name: The Upward Spiral
2816.description: You begin your ascent into the upper levels of the tower, sweating and muttering under your breath as step after step passes beneath your boots. Tiny alcoves line the walls, each contains a small earthenware dish of some kind of glowing powder which serves to light the tower. After a long while, you stop to catch your breath and habitually glance back to see if anything is following you. To your amazement, you find that the stairs beneath you have disappeared. Apparently whoever built this tower wants you to continue to the top.

2817.name: Tower Top
2817.description: The long series of steps finally terminates in this enormous landing. A magnificent dome of some translucent substance affords a view of the velvety black sky overhead and magnificent works of colored glass have been placed on various pedestals which dot the room.

npcs.name: Vahlin the Astronomer
npcs.description: Vahlin seems absorbed with determining the weight of the sky and will not tolerate any interruptions while undertaking a task of such

2794.name: Display Hall
2794.description: It strikes you as odd that Hashak would place such a magnificent room so deep within his house, far away from the well traveled halls. Gleaming suits of magnificent armor are fixed firmly to the walls (much to your chagrin), and bronze replicas of Alestar's generals line the walls in silent glory. An iron maiden graces the northern wall and numerous gargoyles are frozen in fearsome poses. The only apparent way out of here is back up the steps.

2795.name: Within the Maiden
2795.description: You squeeze into the iron maiden and peer about in the darkness. Glancing down, you notice a small flight of neatly carved steps leading far below the house. The faint sound of rushing water reaches your ears from below.

d.to_room_name: River Beneath the House of Hashak

2811.name: In the Brush
2811.description: You stand in a tiny clearing, shielded by the springy orange bushes that grow at its edges. Although the sky and air retain their unusual texture here, this part of the Arboretum feels somehow different. . .

2812.name: The Arboretum
2812.description: This accursed maze of trees seems to wind on forever in one single, convoluted line. Waves of despair again wash over you as you wonder if you will ever make it out of here alive and return to the world that you once knew.

2813.name: Base of the Tower
2813.description: Stepping out of a cluster of trees, you stand stock still for a moment, thunderstruck by the sudden appearance of a massive stone tower shuddering up from the earth and clawing at the sky. Heart racing with anticipation, you slowly circle the tower and find the large stone doorway set into the front to be the only entrance.

2814.name: Bottom of the Stone Steps
2814.description: Here within the tower, the air is damp and has a slightly musty smell to it. The tower is obviously not deserted, as the floor is well swept and a cheery fire burns with a strange greenish light in a small alcove to the east. A spiraling stone stairway leads up to the second floor, and a solid-looking stone door blocks the entrance.

2819.name: Focus of Power
2819.description: Thrusting the boulder aside and leaping into the room, you are immediately taken aback by the flows of raw power that twist and thrash about the room, emanating from a swirling vortex that churns on the eastern wall. Howls and wails resound from within the portal, and shrieking winds seek to tear you off your feet and hurl you against the wall.

npcs.name: Ghost of hashak
npcs.description: His forearms and biceps buckle into deeply defined and powerful cords, and he pulls a gruesome warhammer from a loop at his belt. . .

npcs.name: Hashak's familiar
npcs.description: protrude from the plates which cover the thing's body.  With a malevolent grin, it leaps upward and prepares to strike.

2787.name: Withered Iron Gates
2787.description: You have happened upon the gates to a magnificent house set deep within the forest. Things have obviously deteriorated since the time of its creation, and the gates are no exception. Long ago they must have stood firm, barring entry to the great house, but the ravages of time have long since reft them of their strength. The hinges are cracked and rusted with disuse, and a small sign attached to a pole driven into the wall creaks in the wind.

2788.name: Crumbling Courtyard
2788.description: Low walls made of a crumbling stone partition the courtyard into several smaller sections choked with rocks and weeds. Great statues and hideous gargoyles claw at the air, grim reminders of the brutality of Hashak and the lust for power that drove him insane. Glancing about, you can perceive exits only to the north and south.

npcs.name: Ensorcelled gargoyle
npcs.description: powerful enchantments have given it life to guard the house of Hashak.

2789.name: The Grand Porch
2789.description: Gaudy furnishings lay crumbling on this great stone porch, giving mute testimony to the elevated opinion that Hashak held of himself. A massive door, still standing after years of inattention, bars passage into the house. With great trepidation, you reach up and bring the iron knocker smashing down upon the lion's head set into the door and shiver as a hollow booming reverberates within the house for a time, and then subsides into an eerie silence.

npcs.name: Ghostly hound
npcs.description: ghostly hound.  Chilling green eyes meet yours, and the beast begins licking its chops at the thought of feasting on your soul.

2790.name: Entryway
2790.description: Glancing about, you are amazed at how well the inside of the house has stood up. Sealed against the elements by the house's stout construction, the furniture and carpeting bear little sign of the centuries of disuse and inattention. A large tapestry still hangs on the southern wall, and a short hallway runs east.

npcs.name: Spiritual servant
npcs.description: This tiny ghost is clad in a small waistcoat.  He nods somewhat distantly and gives a small bow, a conditioned response from a time long

2791.name: Main Hall
2791.description: Plush red carpeting imported from Alestar's holdings far to the north sinks slowly beneath your feet as you stride down the hall. Beautiful paintings depicting the short but spectacular conquest of the Talrynians line the walls and well upholstered couches have been placed where the hallway turns. Rounding a final bend, a large painting of Hashak catches your eye.

2792.name: A Circular Stone Room
2792.description: The icy stone floor of this desolate room seems to emanate waves of cold, and the frigid walls bear no decoration of any sort. Teeth chattering, you notice a wood framed doorway to the west and a series of stone steps descending into darkness below.

2793.name: Servants Quarters
2793.description: Rows upon rows of neat little bunks are lined up against the walls. Numerous fireplaces have been stacked high with peat blocks; they burn with a strangely cold flame. Fascinated by the effect, you tentatively pass your hand through the flames and marvel at the lack of any sensation.

2806.name: Main Hall
2806.description: The red carpeting ends here, and a small doorway has been set into the southern wall. A magnificent mirror, over fifteen feet across, is hung at the end of the hallway.

2807.name: Stone Path
2807.description: Small grey stones, cunningly cut and fit together, form a smooth path leading away from the house. A small doorway at the north end of the path allows access to Hashak's abode, and a large arch is barely visible to the east.

2808.name: The Flowered Arch
2808.description: The arch, covered with white plaster and twining vines, is somehow still standing after all these years. There must be some very powerful magics at work here to keep such a fragile structure in perfect condition, and it would obviously be wise to mind your step around here. The stone path lies to the west, or you may enter the arch if you are so inclined.

2809.name: The Arboretum
2809.description: Innumerable species of trees are scattered about the area, vying for space in this strange limbo. You find yourself longing to find some recognizable plant which you might identify with and relate to the world of men and beasts. The trees seem to close in on you and sap your strength away, and the desire to simply collapse and remain here is almost irresistable.

2810.name: The Arboretum
2810.description: Deep within the Arboretum, you have lost all sense of time and direction. Escape seems hopeless, and you feel that you will walk these groves until the end of time. Collapsing next to a spongy orange bush, you set your pack up you to try.

2815.name: A Small Landing
2815.description: You stand in a small, circular room midway up the tower. A large pentacle etched into the floor seems to emanate waves of evil, and the iron braziers attached to the walls glow with a cold blue fire. Stairs lead up and down, and a strange rounded boulder seems to block an exit to the east.
